title: Knowledge export framing for future recall

messages.content: For summary mode, do not just preserve chronology mechanically. A future human needs to understand which moves changed the thread and why they mattered.
That implies Important Moves should not be a raw timeline. Each move should say what changed and why it mattered for future recall.
Right, and Reusable Snippets should feel like note-ready anchors: a file, command, API, or pattern plus a short reuse note.
Agreed. We'll keep headings fixed as TL;DR, Problem Frame, Important Moves, Reusable Snippets, Next Steps, and Tags, but anchor Important Moves around move plus why-it-mattered.
Please preserve the concrete refs too: compactComposer.ts, summaryComposer.ts, and exportCompression.ts.
Those will be preserved as reusable references, along with the note that summary is for future human recall rather than agent execution continuity.

gold.reference:
## TL;DR
- The thread tightened Summary so Important Moves explain both what changed and why it mattered, while Reusable Snippets remain note-ready anchors for future human recall.

## Problem Frame
- Summary should not preserve chronology mechanically.
- A future reader needs a compact reconstruction of the thread's meaningful turns, plus grounded references that can be reused without rereading the whole conversation.

## Important Moves
- Move: Reframe `Important Moves` away from raw timeline narration.
  Why it mattered: This makes the summary useful for future human recall instead of just mirroring transcript order.
- Move: Define `Reusable Snippets` as grounded note-ready anchors such as files, commands, APIs, or patterns with short reuse notes.
  Why it mattered: This turns summary output into a usable knowledge artifact rather than a loose recap.

## Reusable Snippets
- Reference: frontend/src/lib/prompts/export/compactComposer.ts
  Reuse: Useful when checking how AI Handoff is currently framed at the composer layer.
- Reference: frontend/src/lib/prompts/export/summaryComposer.ts
  Reuse: Useful when comparing note-ready wording and section anchoring for Summary.
- Reference: frontend/src/sidepanel/utils/exportCompression.ts
  Reuse: Useful when checking runtime validation and fallback context.

## Next Steps
- Add concrete exemplars to the shipped Summary composer prompt.
- Re-check whether Kimi follows `Important Moves` more reliably once the exemplar is in place.

## Tags
- knowledge-export, future-recall, important-moves, reusable-snippets, export-compression
